title: RTK token proxy + Tray API integration
description: Hook-based CLI command rewriting for token savings. Includes Tray API plugin, context7 documentation server, and Portuguese best-practices guide for code quality, TDD, and agent patterns.
overview:
## Claude Code Setup Overview

A productivity-focused configuration built around token efficiency, structured workflows, and deep agent capabilities.

**superpowers** enforces disciplined skill-driven development with brainstorming, planning, TDD, and parallel agent dispatching built in. **claude-mem** provides cross-session persistent memory so context about projects, preferences, and decisions survives between conversations. **token-optimizer** audits and scores context quality, surfacing where tokens are wasted across sessions. **RTK (Rust Token Killer)** is a CLI proxy that transparently rewrites common commands through a hook, cutting 60–90% of token overhead on dev operations. **snapshot** enables exporting and restoring the full Claude Code setup as a portable bundle, making this configuration shareable and reproducible. **claude-hud** adds a live statusline showing model, session cost, context quality, and other runtime signals. **context7** pulls current library and framework documentation on demand so answers reflect the latest APIs, not stale training data. **frontend-design** guides production-grade UI creation with high design standards baked into the workflow. **playwright** gives Claude browser automation for testing and verifying frontend changes end-to-end. **github** connects Claude to GitHub for PR, issue, and repository operations. **plugin-dev** supports building and validating new Claude Code plugins from within the same environment. **tray-api** integrates with the Tray platform for workflow and automation contexts.

Someone would mirror this setup to get a self-improving, token-conscious agent environment with persistent memory, reproducible configuration, and structured development discipline out of the box.
